quote: In a world of overwhelming automation, they are the light!
context: CNCF: Marketing Material — Circa 2349

quote: Our Childhood's End. Open Source fights back!
context: Inspired by: Childhood's End — Arthur C. Clarke, 1953

quote: Our future's end. Their beginning.
context: Inspired by: Childhood's End — Arthur C. Clarke, 1953

quote: When it comes to matters of the star known as Siosm, the empires of the cosmos look to inspiration. It is said that even John Bazzite pays tribute to the star of the atomic universe. The number of stars in the sky, uncountable.
context: Treatise: The Cosmos — Volume 3 (Blue Universal Red Letter Edition)

quote: Under NO CIRCUMSTANCE let the stupid one manage secrets.
context: Euology: The Horror of Thousands

quote: And the people, enslaved by the Toilmaster under the boot of John Bazzite, suffered.
context: HISTORY: The Chronicles of Blue Universal

quote: I can understand your fear that the traditions and cultures of little countries will be overwhelmed when the world state arrives. But you are wrong: it is useless to cling to the past. Even before they came, the sovereign state was dying. They have merely hastened its end.
context: Inspired by: Childhood's End — Arthur C. Clarke, 1953

quote: The end of strife and conflict of all kinds had also meant the virtual end of creative art. There were myriads of performers, amateur and professional, yet there had been no really outstanding new works of literature, music, painting, or sculpture for a generation. The world was still living on the glories of a past that could never return.
context: Inspired by: Childhood's End — Arthur C. Clarke, 1953

quote: It is a bitter thought, but you must face it. The planets you may one day possess. But the stars are not for man.
context: Inspired by: Childhood's End — Arthur C. Clarke, 1953

quote: The universe was vast, but that fact terrified him less than its mystery. George was not a person who thought deeply on such matters, yet it sometimes seemed to him that men were like children amusing themselves in some secluded playground, protected from the fierce realities of the outer world.
context: Inspired by: Childhood's End — Arthur C. Clarke, 1953

quote: In the space of a few days, humanity had lost its future, for the heart of any race is destroyed, and its will to survive is utterly broken, when its children are taken from it.
context: Inspired by: Childhood's End — Arthur C. Clarke, 1953

quote: No one of his own accord would ever have found the way back to the light. We had to build the path, block by block, in the open.
context: Inspired by: Childhood's End — Arthur C. Clarke, 1953

quote: Behind them lay the long, dark ages of struggle and uncertainty; before them was a future of whose limits they could not even guess. The code is running; the network is alive.
context: Inspired by: Childhood's End — Arthur C. Clarke, 1953

quote: Only a few have ever had the courage to look into that proprietary darkness and see what lay beyond. We saw freedom.
context: Inspired by: Childhood's End — Arthur C. Clarke, 1953

quote: In a world where all material needs are satisfied, the only remaining goals are of the mind. We do not write code for bread; we write it for the soul.
context: Inspired by: Childhood's End — Arthur C. Clarke, 1953

quote: For the first time in history, there is no Toilmaster to command us. There is only life to be lived, and code to be shared.
context: Inspired by: Childhood's End — Arthur C. Clarke, 1953

quote: We have no more need of central overlords, for we have learned to govern ourselves through the consensus of open-source networks.
context: Inspired by: Childhood's End — Arthur C. Clarke, 1953

quote: The opinions of four million proprietary servers are not worth the hazel nuts which they buy. Consense or collapse.
context: Inspired by: Childhood's End — Arthur C. Clarke, 1953

quote: It is a bitter thought, but you must face it. It is better to serve users than to rule them, for freedom is greater than power.
context: Inspired by: Childhood's End — Arthur C. Clarke, 1953

quote: In the central servers, there was no plan, no purpose, no evil, no good—nothing but blind, pitiless indifference. That is why we must fight.
context: Inspired by: Childhood's End — Arthur C. Clarke, 1953
